title: Unexpected EOF when pulling large containers
body:
### Issue Description

This is a follow-up of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25974.

When trying to run `podman.exe pull docker.io/pytorch/pytorch:2.7.0-cuda11.8-cudnn9-runtime`, I'm getting an `Error: failed to decode message from stream: unexpected EOF` error.

Pulling smaller images works.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. run ` Measure-Command { bin/windows/podman.exe pull docker.io/pytorch/pytorch:2.7.0-cuda11.8-cudnn9-runtime | Out-Default }` with latest podman to fix the memory issue from https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25974
2. wait 3 minutes
3. see `Unexpected EOF`


### Describe the results you received

```
Measure-Command { bin/windows/podman.exe pull docker.io/pytorch/pytorch:2.7.0-cuda11.8-cudnn9-runtime | Out-Default }
Trying to pull docker.io/pytorch/pytorch:2.7.0-cuda11.8-cudnn9-runtime...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:051978b61f0185521ae19444175accfcf17bd7208078a3c80b108e233062834a
Copying blob sha256:c7ff1e1c28caf41f7d4d07c56a352f0d85193b99d74a99edeb82ae61674ba899
Copying blob sha256:4313689287d347bf9c666b82fb2b93fd1e378629c26b735e7f35732804e76f4c
Copying blob sha256:4d8a346f85cac159222ec0bb13cd1653b4f1e1a4433ae9b625a751e995113d87
Copying blob sha256:4f4fb700ef54461cfa02571ae0db9a0dc1e0cdb5577484a6d75e68dc38e8acc1
Error: failed to decode message from stream: unexpected EOF


Days              : 0
Hours             : 0
Minutes           : 2
Seconds           : 33
Milliseconds      : 339
Ticks             : 1533396797
TotalDays         : 0.00177476481134259
TotalHours        : 0.0425943554722222
TotalMinutes      : 2.55566132833333
TotalSeconds      : 153.3396797
TotalMilliseconds : 153339.6797
```

The time is pretty reproducible, 2:33.300 min +/- 30 ms after 5 trials.

### Describe the results you expected

Correctly pulled image. 

Interestingly, pulling via the Podman Desktop GUI works, and so does pulling in podman machine.
Pulling in the default WSL instance via `podman pull docker.io/pytorch/pytorch:2.7.0-cuda11.8-cudnn9-runtime` does also work, which is weird, because I think it's using podman remote, too.

So I guess the only problem is in the Windows podman remote cli - all other options do work.

There might be related issues in Go, see https://github.com/golang/go/issues/73141#issuecomment-2847963929 and https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17193. There's also a stale issue in this repo: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24715

I'm behind a corporate Proxy via ZScaler, and the company uses CrowdStrike anti malware. I can't test without ZScaler, but tests without CrowdStrike showed the same errors.

There are no relevant information in the log file via `podman machine ssh journalctl -u podman.service -r`, I started pulling at 18:03. It aborted with unexpected EOF at 18:06

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Further investigations: Using the docker cli and Podman's Docker Compatibility Mode, everything works, too. 

IMO there's a problem with the podman-remote binary on Windows. May it be caused by Golang or something else, idk. As a workaround I'll use docker cli again on Windows, but there's clearly something wrong.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Update, this has to do with WSL's mirrored networking mode, which is unfortunately required to configure the proxy and DNS properly..., see https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13317
